frameworks.name: five-factor model of personality
frameworks.one_line: FFM personality traits as the target dimension to relate to brain connectivity

frameworks.name: intrinsic functional connectome
frameworks.one_line: whole-brain resting-state functional connectivity as the neural substrate

frameworks.name: intersubject similarity analysis
frameworks.one_line: pairwise similarity between subjects links network and trait structure

frameworks.name: twin-based heritability
frameworks.one_line: MZ-vs-DZ contrast used to assess heritability of connectivity

core_question: Is interindividual similarity in personality traits reflected in similarity of intrinsic whole-brain functional connectivity?
key_assumptions: personality is well-captured by the five-factor model
resting-state functional connectivity indexes stable individual traits
intersubject similarity is a meaningful comparison metric for both traits and networks
manipulations: compared MZ vs DZ twin pairs to probe heritability
varied subject pairs along personality-similarity continuum
key_findings: identified a global personality network in resting-state connectivity related to FFM traits
connectivity strength of this network is more similar in MZ than DZ twins (heritable)
across subject pairs, network similarity correlates positively with personality similarity
global personality network predicts individual item-level questionnaire responses
interpretations: supports a 'similar brain, similar personality' hypothesis linking trait and connectome structure
personality traits have a heritable neural signature in intrinsic functional architecture
